message:
fix(pager): make PARALLEL_WAL_LANE_TEST_LOCK robust to panicking tests

The 7 sites that acquire PARALLEL_WAL_LANE_TEST_LOCK used .lock().unwrap(),
which panics on PoisonError. When any of the parallel-WAL tests panic
mid-hold (e.g. on a transient test-harness race), every subsequent test
that touches this lock then panics too — cascading a single real
failure into 10+ "test_parallel_wal_*" failures.

Replace with .unwrap_or_else(std::sync::PoisonError::into_inner); the
standard pattern for test-only mutexes. The lock is just a Mutex<()>
whose only purpose is serial-access ordering; there is no protected
invariant a poisoned guard could break.

Isolated runs of the affected tests already pass:
  test_parallel_wal_lane_reuse_after_worker_churn                   ok
  test_parallel_wal_concurrent_writers_on_disjoint_lanes_...        ok

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
